auditions_what_to_prepare: Come prepared with a 1 minute monologue, and to play a few theatre games with the rest of the audition group.
auditions_fees: $350 for each production the actor is cast.
lineup_blurb: Performing at Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts, Second Stage.
auditions_strip_text: Auditioning for the next Stories on Stage company?
intro_lede: Beloved children's books brought to life in short, lively performances designed for young audiences. Six productions a year, performed by our annual Stories on Stage company. Buy tickets for public performances, or contact us for school bookings.
auditions_intro: One annual audition fills the Stories on Stage company. Cast members appear in 1–3 productions across the year, depending on casting and calendar conflict dates performers provide up front.
title: Stories on Stage
auditions_form_blurb: Registration includes a calendar conflicts form so we can cast around your family's schedule. Please complete this before the audition date.
explainer_body:
Stories on Stage is PYT's program for adapting beloved children's literature into short, accessible stage performances. Each production runs about 35–45 minutes — long enough to tell a real story, short enough for our youngest audience members to stay engaged.

The annual company casts about 16–20 young performers (ages 8–18) through a single audition each September. Cast members don't appear in every production — instead, each actor is cast in 1–3 shows across the year, based on the role fit and on calendar conflicts you tell us about up front. This gives our young actors a real season's worth of experience, while keeping the weekly rehearsal commitment manageable. Rehearsals generally run on Tuesday and Sunday evenings, for 5-6 weeks. 

Stories on Stage performs at Mountain View Center for the Performing Arts' Second Stage. Performances for school audiences take place on one Friday morning per production, and for the public on one Saturday per production.
